summarization.a: The line chart displays the thermal desorption profile of titanium fluoride species. Significant ion current peaks are observed for TiF, TiF2, TiF3 and TiF4.
summarization.b: TThe line chart displays the thermal desorption profile of titanium fluoride species. Significant ion current peaks are observed only for TiF,  TiF2, TiF3 and TiF4 are showing negligible signal intensity.

data_extraction.b:
| m/z + species | temperature (°C) | Ion current, (pA) | 
|---|---|---|
| m/z 67 TiF | 376 | 0.16 |
| m/z 67 TiF | 715  | 0.05 |

a.question: Which species have two desorption peaks?
What features of the spectrum support the conclusion that there are two distinct types of titanium fluorides?
a.answer: There are two distinct temperature peaks (at ~350°C and ~450°C). The chemical composition of each peak is also different. The first type (~350°C) dissociates into TiF, TiF₂, TiF₃, and TiF₄, and the second type (~450°C) into TiF and TiF₃. . These differences are the evidence that the sample contains two  chemically distinct forms of titanium fluoride.

b.question: Were titanium fluorides completely removed with O2 plasma or not? What supports this conclusion?
b.answer: No. While the significant desorption peaks for TiF₂, TiF₃ and TiF₄ disappeared, TiF still shows a small residual signal after the O₂ plasma treatment. It is indicating that titanium fluorides were not removed completely.
